_comment: Regression baseline for the design-guidelines task suite. The `passing` array lists scenario IDs that are known to pass on this branch; `count` MUST match its length. run_scenarios.sh exits with code 3 if the live passing-count drops below `count`. Tighten this floor (raise the count, add IDs) only when a clean run actually proves the additions pass. NEVER lower the floor to make a failing task green — that is the regression we are trying to prevent.
_last_refreshed_commit: 7c62a6c — full --visual run in remote container 2026-07-10: 39/42 pass. Prior file falsely listed T23_end_phase_position, T27_end_phase_refactor and T45_final_audit as passing; their required implementation (EndPhaseButton node + t23_anchor_offset()) has never existed in git history, so those entries cannot have come from a real run. Floor corrected to the measured 39 — this is a record fix, not a floor-lowering to green a regression.
_refresh_policy: Bump only after a clean `bash 40k/tests/run_scenarios.sh --visual` (visual baseline) AND `bash 40k/tests/run_scenarios.sh --sp` (sp suite passes). T45 (final design-guidelines audit) is the canonical refresh point.
_environment_note: Fresh container? Run `godot --headless --import` FROM 40k/ (or with --path 40k) once to populate .godot/global_script_class_cache.cfg — required for class_name resolution. Without this, every autoload referencing a class_name (GameConstants, FactionPalettes, …) fails to parse and ScenarioRunner never activates.
